prevalence.trend: stable, with increasing awareness in urban areas

publicHealthSystem.screeningCoverage: Low; no routine national screening, primarily through primary care consultations
publicHealthSystem.controlRate: 35% adequately managed, limited by access disparities

regionalSpecificities.treatmentProtocol: Acute: triptans, NSAIDs via SUS; Prophylaxis: beta-blockers, amitriptyline, topiramate
regionalSpecificities.regionalChallenges: Limited triptan access in public system (SUS), neurologist shortage in rural areas, high disability burden among women
regionalSpecificities.strongPoints: SBCe guidelines, basic medications via SUS, growing telemedicine options
